fields.description:
## Why
This is the dependency root of the oracle. The three-valued evidence contract, the fail-open execution harness,
and the optional-extra scaffolding are shared by every engine â€” they must exist and be stable before Engine A,
T0, or Engine B are built. Getting the contract right here is what makes "one implementation, not two" real.

## What
- The normalized **evidence model**: `resolved | abstained(reason)` with a CLOSED structured reason enum
  (`unsupported_lang | no_tool | parse_error | timeout | ambiguous | private_or_internal_suspected |
  network_error | rate_limited | version_skew | invalid_detector | other`), provenance/tier, and a coverage
  record. `version_skew` (the #1 real failure) and `invalid_detector` (a project detector failing validation)
  are first-class; `other` is the explicit catch-all (no open `â€¦`). Resolution-lane resolved value is `refuted`
  (never an asserted absence). One internal finding shape; SARIF 2.1.0 map at the edges (superset).
- The **fail-open recover/timeout harness** every backend runs inside. OUT-OF-PROCESS tools (ctags, ast-grep,
  OpenGrep, scc/lizard, registry HTTP) use the subprocess reaper (mirror `acli_subprocess`: process-group,
  SIGTERMâ†’SIGKILL, structured timeout). IN-PROCESS bindings (tree-sitter) CANNOT be bounded by a thread/signal
  timeout (it can't interrupt a hung C-extension call, and a segfault would kill the host) â€” so prefer the CLI
  form of every matcher, and run any unavoidable in-process binding inside a WORKER SUBPROCESS so the same reaper
  applies. A crash/timeout/missing-tool/version-skew becomes a recorded `abstain`, never raises; the visible skip
  IS the coverage record â€” never a silent no-op. Each backend's version is pinned/recorded.
- **Optional-extra scaffolding**: a new extra in `pyproject.toml`, `_optional.py` guard + `guard_import`, core
  stays import-clean (the existing optionality CI gate), non-adopting clients import nothing.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/grounding/` (new package) â€” contract + harness + extra wiring only; no backend logic.
- Polyglot/optional by construction; no Python-specific assumptions in the contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Evidence model + CLOSED structured reason enum (incl. version_skew, invalid_detector, other) + coverage implemented; `refute`/`abstain` representable; SARIF map both directions.
- [ ] Fail-open harness covers OUT-OF-PROCESS (subprocess reaper) AND IN-PROCESS (worker-subprocess) backends: crash / hang / timeout / missing tool / version-skew â†’ recorded `abstain`, never raises â€” incl. a segfaulting C-extension parse.
- [ ] Optional extra added; core import-clean (optionality CI gate passes); non-adopting client imports nothing.
- [ ] Unit-tested: harness converts each failure mode to the correct `abstain(reason)` + coverage entry.
